title: stack.rs: send_coap_raw passes payload slice with confusing variable name
description:
File: rust/lichen-node/src/stack.rs:345-354

The variable `l2_payload_len` is named as if it's a length (usize), but it's actually a slice (&[u8]):

```rust
let mut l2_payload = [0u8; 201];
let l2_payload_len = wrap_schc_payload(&schc[..schc_len], &mut l2_payload)?;  // Returns &[u8]

let wire_len = self
    .link
    .build_frame(self.epoch, seqnum, &[], l2_payload_len, &mut wire)
```

This is confusing and could lead to bugs during refactoring if someone assumes it's a usize length.

Fix: Rename to `l2_payload` or `l2_payload_slice` to match its actual type.
close_reason: Fixed: renamed l2_payload_len to l2_data in both send_coap_raw and send_ipv6_raw functions to accurately reflect the variable type (slice, not length)
